name: Potato Cauliflower Curry
tagline: Tomato-based potato and cauliflower curry with ginger and cumin
highlight: Pantry-staple curry, no eggs, all warmth

hero.alt: Aloo gobi with potato, cauliflower, and tomato gravy
hero.caption: potato cauliflower — gravy version

healthFacts: Cauliflower is a cruciferous source of glucosinolates — anti-inflammatory.
Cooking turmeric with black pepper boosts curcumin absorption ~20×.

steps.title: Temper cumin
steps.detail: Heat 3 tbsp oil in a wide pan. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter, ~20 sec.
steps.tip: Hot oil first, then seeds — cold-oil-cold-seeds gives weak flavor.

steps.title: Brown onion and bloom spices
steps.detail: Add onion, cook 8 min until golden. Stir in ginger-garlic paste 1 min. Add turmeric, coriander, salt — bloom 30 sec.
steps.tip: Onions should look mahogany before tomato goes in — that depth carries the curry.

steps.title: Tomato then potato
steps.detail: Add tomato puree; cook 5 min until oil separates. Add potatoes and 1 cup water. Cover, simmer 8 min.
steps.tip: Watch for the oil to ooze to the surface — that's the sign your masala is cooked.

steps.title: Cauliflower and finish
steps.detail: Add cauliflower, cover, cook 12 min until both veg are fork-tender. Finish with garam masala and cilantro.
steps.tip: Garam masala goes at the end so its volatile aromatics don't burn off.

utensils.name: Wide pan or kadhai with lid
